events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need assess hazard: anchor point inadequate, guessing at 5000-lb rating - TRD (tie-off device?). In residential job, fall protection anchor must support 5000 lbs per worker attached, or be designed as part of complete personal fall arrest system that maintains safety factor of at least two, under supervision of qualified person. If anchor point is inadequate and only guessed, it's non-compliant, installed-defective. Work in progress. Decision fail. Risk high/critical? Inadequate anchor for fall arrest can lead to fatality if fall occurs. Severity critical maybe high. Need immediate controls: stop work, remove workers from fall hazard, inspect/test/verify anchor capacity by qualified person, install proper anchor per manufacturer/OSHA, use temporary alternative fall protection (e.g., guardrails, scissor lift, ladder safety system) until verified, training. References: OSHA 1926.502(d)(15) anchorages capable of supporting at least 5,000 lbs per worker attached; 1910.140(c)(13) anchorage requirements. Also 1926.501 duty to have fall protection. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 50? Not important. value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty. workable empty. Need valid JSON only. Ensure no prose.

    "Anchor point capacity is unknown and only assumed to be 5,000 lb rather than verified.",
    "Anchor is not confirmed capable of supporting 5,000 lb per attached worker or meeting a qualified-designed PFAS safety factor.",
    "Workers are actively tied off to an unverified anchorage, creating a fall-arrest failure risk."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately in the fall-exposure area.",
    "Remove workers from the fall hazard until a compliant anchor is verified or alternate protection is in place.",
    "Have a competent/qualified person inspect, test, or document the anchor capacity per OSHA and manufacturer requirements.",
    "Install or use a verified 5,000-lb-rated anchor, an engineered PFAS anchor, or substitute fall protection such as guardrails, scaffolding, or an aerial lift.",
    "Brief the crew on proper anchor selection and prohibit tying off to unverified points."
  ],
  "rationale": "An unverified anchor cannot be trusted to arrest a fall. OSHA requires anchorages to support at least 5,000 lb per attached worker or be designed as part of a complete PFAS by a qualified person. Guessing the rating exposes workers to potentially fatal failure, so the work fails inspection and must be corrected before continuing.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M - Fall Protection",
    "OSHA 1926.502(d)(15) - Anchorage strength requirements",
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(13) - Personal fall protection system anchorage requirements"
  ]
